guan@shanyuegroup.com

Email inquiry
Contact use

When to contact Lucky Number Guide

Use the contact page for content corrections, broken links, report delivery questions, partnership inquiries, and product notes that fit the site. A useful message includes the page URL, the number or topic involved, and a short description of the issue. If you are asking about a paid report, include the report link, order ID if available, and the email address used at checkout.

Do not send passwords, payment credentials, private identity documents, medical records, legal documents, or sensitive financial information. The contact channel is not a professional advisory service and cannot review urgent safety, medical, legal, financial, or mental health situations. For those matters, contact qualified services first.

Correction notes are welcome. Number meanings can vary across languages, dialects, regions, and family habits, so good corrections should explain whether the issue is translation, cultural context, wording, broken navigation, or a factual error. Partnership messages should be specific about the product type and should avoid exaggerated claims about luck, wealth, guaranteed success, or spiritual protection.

Support expectations

What happens after you send a message

Most messages can be handled by checking the relevant page, confirming a report link, correcting a phrase, or clarifying a product note. If a reader asks for a personalized number interpretation outside the available tool or report flow, the response may point them back to the calculator, meanings page, or guide library. The contact channel is not a live fortune service.

For product or affiliate inquiries, the site prefers practical items such as printable reports, number cards, study sheets, journals, tasteful charms, or cultural learning tools. Products that promise guaranteed wealth, romantic control, medical protection, gambling success, or spiritual rescue are not a good fit. Keep the message specific and include enough context to evaluate the request without sending sensitive private information.

Response scope

What the contact page cannot provide

Messages cannot provide private divination, emergency advice, legal review, medical guidance, financial planning, or guaranteed number recommendations. If you need help choosing between several numbers, include the use case and the numbers you are considering, but keep the question practical. The best support request asks for clarification, delivery help, or a content correction rather than certainty about the future.
